exercises.title: Run BFS and DFS on a sample graph
exercises.description: Visit the same graph with BFS and DFS and compare the visitation order.
exercises.expected_outcome: BFS should visit by layers while DFS should go deep along one path.

exercises.title: Compare graph representations
exercises.description: Store the same graph as an adjacency list and adjacency matrix and compare memory use.
exercises.expected_outcome: Sparse graphs should be much cheaper to store as adjacency lists.

exercises.title: Implement Dijkstra
exercises.description: Write a shortest-path solver for nonnegative weighted graphs using a priority queue.
exercises.expected_outcome: The solver should return minimum distances from the source to every node.

exercises.title: Implement topological sort
exercises.description: Compute a dependency order for a DAG and detect cycles when the order is impossible.
exercises.expected_outcome: Every edge should point from an earlier node to a later node in the final order.

exercises.title: Build Kruskal with union-find
exercises.description: Implement minimum spanning tree construction with disjoint-set union and test it on weighted graphs.
exercises.expected_outcome: The selected edges should connect all nodes with minimum total weight and no cycles.

exercises.title: Solve weighted grid paths with A*
exercises.description: Build an A* pathfinder on a grid and compare it with Dijkstra on the same map.
exercises.expected_outcome: A* should explore fewer nodes when the heuristic is admissible.
